title: Public browser Simulation Worker session bridge
summary: Expose a reviewed package-root browser Worker/session bridge so apps/web can issue WM-0150 playable commands to the authoritative Simulation Worker without deep imports or shell-local authority.
acceptance: A public package-root API from @wuming-town/sim-worker exposes a browser Worker/session client or equivalent root-exported entry URL/factory; apps/web never imports @wuming-town/sim-worker subpaths or packages/sim-worker/src/* internals

apps/web has a reviewed manifest boundary to consume @wuming-town/sim-worker from the package root only, and does not add @wuming-town/sim-core, sim-core internals, platform-owned simulation authority or any new runtime dependency

The browser session bridge can initialize the WM-0150 playable command scenario, send PlayerCommandBatch messages for PrioritizeLampWork and QueueSimpleBuild, and receive authoritative Ready, CommandResult, RenderSnapshot, UiDelta and MetricsSample output from the Simulation Worker

The bridge is transport/read-model delivery only: it does not mutate world state, synthesize command acceptance, invent blocked reasons, assign pawns, create jobs, reserve resources, complete builds or keep authoritative UI caches

Reliable Worker messages such as CommandResult, SaveReady and FatalSimulationError are not dropped by render snapshot backpressure; lifecycle and destroy behavior are explicit and do not rely on hidden global singletons

Save/load stance is explicit: no public save compatibility or migration promise is added; in-progress playable command persistence remains unsupported or fail-closed with structured reasons until a separate versioned save task is approved
Worker smoke or focused browser tests prove a real Chromium module Worker is reached through the public root bridge, and package boundary checks prove there are no deep imports
The report states the exact public import/API path WM-0152 should use when it resumes, plus any remaining limitations for Web save/load and product UI wiring

history.details: Created to unblock WM-0152 after confirming apps/web has no legal public browser Simulation Worker/session bridge.

saveLoadStance: No public save compatibility is introduced. The bridge may expose Worker RequestSave/LoadSession transport status, but Web must not persist or restore in-progress playable command state until a separate versioned save/load task is approved.
forbiddenPaths: apps/web importing @wuming-town/sim-worker subpaths or packages/sim-worker/src/* internals
apps/web depending on @wuming-town/sim-core or sim-core internals
product HUD, placement UI or localization implementation beyond a focused bridge smoke adapter
UI-owned authoritative world, job, pawn, reservation, resource, build or blocked-reason state
fake shell-local gameplay claimed as authoritative
new public save compatibility, migration or release promises
new runtime dependencies without a separate ADR
public release, Early Access, store, signing, telemetry, accounts, paid services or public feedback surfaces
